decisions.title: Commit to Python Rules GA by Q2 2026
decisions.context: Multiple Python execution issues blocking production readiness (ENG-9056, ENG-10454). PyRunner pods unstable, completions bug, missing alarms. Need decisive go/no-go for Q2.
decisions.blockedBy: PyRunner stability issues, production monitoring gaps

arguments.for: Python Rules is core to 'Build the Moat' strategy, differentiator vs competitors, customers waiting
arguments.against: Production stability concerns, pod availability issues suggest infrastructure not ready for GA commitment

decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 families feature over scattered enhancements
decisions.context: Four parallel tickets on families support (ENG-6411, 6405, 6412, ENG-5808 duplicates). Signals fragmented work across chart types instead of cohesive release.

arguments.for: Families unlock 'Nail the Core' for multi-asset workflows, drives Decisions Captured metric by enabling cross-run comparison
arguments.against: Current approach allows parallel progress, single release risks延迟 if any component blocks

decisions.title: Sunset legacy data layer by Q3 2026
decisions.context: Data layer v2 work ongoing (ENG-10451 Fedramp check, ENG-10282 shadow traffic, ENG-9616 migration off old tables). Need timeline commitment.

arguments.for: Technical debt reducing velocity, migration signals exist, clean cutover enables 'Embed Deeper' with performance gains
arguments.against: Risk to stability during migration, unclear customer impact timeline, Fedramp verification incomplete

decisions.title: Canvas file import formats: breadth vs depth
decisions.context: Three new import formats in flight (ENG-10457 HDF5, ENG-10456 TDMS, plus Parquet work). Spreading thin vs perfecting core formats.

arguments.for: Format diversity drives adoption across verticals (aerospace=TDMS, research=HDF5), increases Decisions Captured denominator
arguments.against: Each format adds maintenance burden, better to perfect Parquet/CSV experience and let users convert upstream

decisions.title: Invest in Ask SIFT documentation infrastructure now
decisions.context: ENG-10461 'flesh out documentation story' suggests gaps. Ask SIFT effectiveness depends on quality docs corpus.

arguments.for: Ask SIFT is 'Build the Moat' differentiator, documentation ROI compounds over time, enables self-service (Embed Deeper)
arguments.against: Engineering-heavy quarter already, documentation historically under-resourced, can start small and iterate

timeAudit.weekSummary: Only 20% free time for strategic work. 3 Python Rules escalations consumed unplanned hours. Families work spreading across 4 devs reduces coordination efficiency.
alerts: Friday 4/18: Customer escalation call scheduled over planned strategy session
Week trend: Decision-making time down 40% vs last month due to production firefighting
